agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A logistics platform introducing automated route optimization for drivers at shift start, with dynamic recalculation triggers, customer notification windows, vehicle class constraints, and configurable stop duration estimates. This spans multiple bounded contexts: route planning, fleet/driver management, delivery lifecycle, and customer notifications.","bounded_context":"Route Optimization — but this feature touches at least four bounded contexts: (1) Route Planning (optimization, recalculation, stop sequencing), (2) Fleet Management (driver shifts, vehicle class licensing, zone assignment), (3) Delivery Management (delivery lifecycle, pending status, failure handling), (4) Customer Notification (delivery proximity alerts). The spec conflates these into one feature description, which is a design risk.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Optimized Route","definition":"An ordered sequence of delivery stops assigned to a driver, calculated to minimize travel time/distance within their zone, respecting vehicle class constraints."},{"term":"Zone","definition":"A geographic area within which deliveries are grouped and assigned to drivers. Zones are the unit of route assignment and stop-time configuration."},{"term":"Shift","definition":"A bounded work period for a driver, beginning when the driver starts work. Triggers initial route calculation."},{"term":"Pending Delivery","definition":"A delivery that has not yet been attempted, is within a zone, and is eligible for assignment to a route."},{"term":"Recalculation Trigger","definition":"An event that forces the route to be re-optimized: new delivery added, delivery marked failed, or driver manual deviation."},{"term":"Next 3 Stops Window","definition":"A proximity notification threshold — the set of the next three upcoming stops on the active route. Customers whose delivery is within this window receive a notification."},{"term":"Licensed Vehicle Class","definition":"A classification of both vehicles and deliveries (e.g., motorcycle, van, freight truck) that constrains which deliveries a driver may be assigned. A driver's vehicle class must be equal to or exceed the delivery's required class."},{"term":"Manual Deviation","definition":"When a driver departs from the system-suggested route order, e.g., skipping a stop or visiting stops out of sequence."},{"term":"Stop Duration Estimate","definition":"The expected time a driver spends at each delivery stop. Defaults to 4 minutes but is overridable per zone by the operations team."},{"term":"Failed Delivery","definition":"A delivery attempt that was unsuccessful (e.g., customer unavailable, access issue), removing it from the active route and triggering recalculation."},{"term":"Driver","definition":"A person operating a vehicle of a specific licensed class, assigned to a zone, who executes an optimized route during their shift."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"When a driver starts their shift, the system must automatically calculate and assign an optimized route from all pending deliveries in the driver's zone.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is operational behavior triggered by a shift event. The optimization algorithm and assignment policy can change without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN pending deliveries exist in a zone WHEN a driver starts a shift in that zone THEN an optimized route containing those deliveries is assigned to the driver."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"The route must be recalculated when a new delivery is added to the driver's zone while their shift is active.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Recalculation policy is a business decision — ops could choose batch recalculation or threshold-based triggers instead.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver has an active route WHEN a new pending delivery is added to their zone THEN the route is recalculated to include the new delivery."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"The route must be recalculated when a delivery on the route is marked as failed.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Recalculation on failure is a policy choice; the system could alternatively just skip the stop.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver has an active route with delivery D WHEN delivery D is marked as failed THEN the route is recalculated excluding delivery D."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"The route must be recalculated when a driver manually deviates from the suggested route.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Deviation handling is a policy — could also just warn the driver instead of recalculating.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver has an active route WHEN the driver visits a stop out of sequence or skips a stop THEN the route is recalculated from the driver's current position."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Customers must be notified when their delivery enters the 'next 3 stops' window on the driver's active route.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The notification threshold (3 stops) is a business preference, not a data integrity constraint. Could be changed to 2 or 5.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver is progressing along their route WHEN a customer's delivery becomes one of the next 3 upcoming stops THEN the customer receives a notification."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"A driver must never be assigned a delivery that exceeds their licensed vehicle class capability.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a hard safety and regulatory constraint. Assigning a motorcycle courier a freight shipment is physically impossible and potentially illegal. Violating this corrupts the route's validity at a fundamental level.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver with vehicle class V WHEN the system builds or recalculates a route THEN no delivery requiring a vehicle class exceeding V is included."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"The default estimated stop duration is 4 minutes per delivery stop.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a configurable operational parameter, explicitly stated as overridable.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN no zone-specific override exists WHEN calculating route ETAs THEN each stop uses a 4-minute estimate."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"The operations team can override the stop duration estimate on a per-zone basis.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an administrative configuration capability.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN the operations team sets stop duration for zone Z to 6 minutes WHEN a route is calculated in zone Z THEN each stop uses a 6-minute estimate."},{"id":"BR-9","rule":"A driver can only be assigned deliveries within their assigned zone.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Zone boundaries are a fundamental partitioning constraint. Cross-zone assignment would violate the system's geographic routing model and potentially labor/contract rules.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver assigned to zone Z WHEN a route is built THEN only deliveries with destination in zone Z are included."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A driver starts their shift and there are 10 pending deliveries in their zone","when":"The shift start event is processed","then":"The system assigns the driver an optimized route containing all 10 deliveries, ordered to minimize travel time/distance"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A driver has an active route with 8 remaining stops","when":"A new delivery is added to their zone","then":"The route is recalculated to include the new delivery in the optimal position, and the driver sees the updated route"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A driver has an active route and delivery #4 is marked as failed","when":"The failure event is processed","then":"The route is recalculated excluding delivery #4, re-optimizing the remaining stops from the driver's current position"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A driver's suggested next stop is Stop B but the driver goes to Stop D instead","when":"The system detects the deviation","then":"The route is recalculated from the driver's current position with remaining undelivered stops"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A driver completes a stop and the next 3 stops are deliveries for customers X, Y, Z","when":"The route advances","then":"Customers X, Y, Z each receive a notification that their delivery is arriving soon"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A motorcycle driver is in zone A and zone A has both parcel deliveries and a freight shipment","when":"The route is calculated","then":"The freight shipment is excluded from the motorcycle driver's route; only parcel deliveries compatible with motorcycle class are assigned"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"The operations team has set zone B stop duration to 6 minutes","when":"A route is calculated for a driver in zone B","then":"ETA calculations use 6 minutes per stop instead of the default 4 minutes"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A driver starts their shift and there are zero pending deliveries in their zone","when":"The shift start event is processed","then":"The driver is assigned an empty route and is available to receive deliveries as they arrive"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A customer's delivery is at position 4 on the route (outside the next-3 window)","when":"The driver completes one more stop, moving that delivery to position 3","then":"The customer receives a notification at this transition point"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A route recalculation occurs and a customer who was in the next-3 window is now at position 5","when":"The recalculated route is finalized","then":"No duplicate notification is sent; the customer's window status is updated accordingly"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Driver starts shift with no pending deliveries in zone","expected_behavior":"Driver receives an empty route. When the first delivery is added to the zone, a route is created and assigned."},{"scenario":"All deliveries on a route are marked as failed","expected_behavior":"Driver has an empty route after final recalculation. Driver status should reflect availability for new assignments."},{"scenario":"Multiple recalculation triggers fire simultaneously (new delivery added + deviation detected)","expected_behavior":"System should coalesce triggers and perform a single recalculation, not two sequential ones, to avoid race conditions and unnecessary recomputation."},{"scenario":"Driver is the only one in a zone and a delivery requiring a higher vehicle class arrives","expected_behavior":"The delivery remains unassigned/pending. It must NOT be force-assigned to violate vehicle class constraints. The system should alert operations."},{"scenario":"Customer notification: delivery oscillates in and out of the next-3 window due to repeated recalculations","expected_behavior":"Customer should receive at most one 'arriving soon' notification per delivery. Duplicate suppression is required."},{"scenario":"Driver deviates but returns to the originally suggested next stop","expected_behavior":"System should still detect the deviation and recalculate (the result may be the same route, but the recalculation must occur to account for time/distance changes)."},{"scenario":"Zone-level stop duration override is removed while a route is active","expected_behavior":"Active routes should either continue with the previous estimate until recalculation, or immediately recalculate with the default 4-minute estimate. Spec does not clarify."},{"scenario":"Two drivers in the same zone start shifts simultaneously","expected_behavior":"Deliveries must be partitioned between the two drivers, not duplicated. The system must handle concurrent route assignment without assigning the same delivery to multiple drivers."},{"scenario":"A delivery is added to a zone boundary — ambiguous zone membership","expected_behavior":"The system must have a deterministic zone resolution rule. A delivery belongs to exactly one zone."},{"scenario":"Driver's shift ends while deliveries remain on route","expected_behavior":"Undelivered stops must be returned to the pending pool for reassignment. They cannot be lost."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Route optimization directly impacts fuel costs, driver labor efficiency, and deliveries-per-shift throughput. A well-implemented system can reduce delivery costs 15-25%. Incorrect vehicle class assignment could result in failed deliveries, SLA penalties, and potential regulatory fines.","operational":"Dynamic recalculation adds system complexity but dramatically improves real-world route quality. The 4-minute default stop estimate needs validation against actual data — a wrong default cascades into every ETA. Operations team override capability is critical for zone-specific realities (urban vs suburban vs industrial areas).","user":"Customers benefit from the next-3-stops notification — it reduces missed deliveries and improves satisfaction. Drivers benefit from automated route planning but may resist if recalculation is too aggressive (constant route changes mid-shift are disorienting). Finding the right recalculation UX is critical.","risk":"Vehicle class constraint violation is the highest-risk failure mode — it is a safety and regulatory issue. Concurrent route assignment (two drivers, same zone) is a data integrity risk. Notification spam from oscillating recalculations is a customer experience risk.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Drivers","impact":"Primary users — route quality directly affects their workday efficiency, earnings (if performance-based), and job satisfaction. Excessive recalculations may cause frustration."},{"group":"Customers","impact":"Receive proximity notifications improving delivery predictability. Risk of notification spam if recalculation edge cases are not handled."},{"group":"Operations Team","impact":"Gains zone-level configuration control over stop duration. Needs alerting for unassignable deliveries (vehicle class mismatch with no eligible driver)."},{"group":"Dispatch / Fleet Management","impact":"Must ensure driver-zone assignments and vehicle class data are accurate, as the system depends on this master data for correct routing."},{"group":"Finance / Business","impact":"Route optimization is a direct cost reduction lever. Measurable KPIs: deliveries per shift, fuel cost per delivery, on-time delivery rate."}]},"confidence":{"level":72,"reasoning":"The spec is relatively clear and well-structured for a product feature description. The core rules are explicit and testable. However, several operational details are unspecified: how deviation is detected, how concurrent multi-driver zone assignment works, notification deduplication policy, and what happens at shift boundaries. These are important implementation details but don't represent contradictions — they are normal gaps in a product spec that need refinement.","high_confidence_areas":["Vehicle class constraint is a hard invariant (BR-6)","Zone-scoped assignment constraint (BR-9)","Recalculation trigger events are clearly enumerated","Stop duration default and override mechanism is well-defined","Next-3-stops notification threshold is explicit"],"low_confidence_areas":["How deviation is detected — GPS-based? Driver self-report? Stop sequence monitoring?","Multi-driver same-zone delivery partitioning strategy","Notification deduplication and suppression rules","Shift end / handoff behavior for undelivered stops","What 'optimized' means algorithmically — shortest distance? Fewest turns? Time windows?","Zone boundary resolution for ambiguous delivery locations"],"assumptions":["Vehicle class is a hierarchical classification where higher classes can handle lower-class deliveries (e.g., a van driver can deliver parcels but not freight)","A driver is assigned to exactly one zone per shift","Zone assignment for deliveries is deterministic and pre-resolved before route calculation","The optimization goal is to minimize total travel time/distance (not explicitly stated)","Notifications are push-based to the customer (channel unspecified)"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}

The spec conflates these into one feature description, which is a design risk.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Optimized Route","definition":"An ordered sequence of delivery stops assigned to a driver, calculated to minimize travel time/distance within their zone, respecting vehicle class constraints."},{"term":"Zone","definition":"A geographic area within which deliveries are grouped and assigned to drivers. Could be changed to 2 or 5.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver is progressing along their route WHEN a customer's delivery becomes one of the next 3 upcoming stops THEN the customer receives a notification."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"A driver must never be assigned a delivery that exceeds their licensed vehicle class capability.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a hard safety and regulatory constraint. Violating this corrupts the route's validity at a fundamental level.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver with vehicle class V WHEN the system builds or recalculates a route THEN no delivery requiring a vehicle class exceeding V is included."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"The default estimated stop duration is 4 minutes per delivery stop.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a configurable operational parameter, explicitly stated as overridable.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN no zone-specific override exists WHEN calculating route ETAs THEN each stop uses a 4-minute estimate."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"The operations team can override the stop duration estimate on a per-zone basis.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an administrative configuration capability.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN the operations team sets stop duration for zone Z to 6 minutes WHEN a route is calculated in zone Z THEN each stop uses a 6-minute estimate."},{"id":"BR-9","rule":"A driver can only be assigned deliveries within their assigned zone.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Zone boundaries are a fundamental partitioning constraint. Cross-zone assignment would violate the system's geographic routing model and potentially labor/contract rules.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver assigned to zone Z WHEN a route is built THEN only deliveries with destination in zone Z are included."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A driver starts their shift and there are 10 pending deliveries in their zone","when":"The shift start event is processed","then":"The system assigns the driver an optimized route containing all 10 deliveries, ordered to minimize travel time/distance"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A driver has an active route with 8 remaining stops","when":"A new delivery is added to their zone","then":"The route is recalculated to include the new delivery in the optimal position, and the driver sees the updated route"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A driver has an active route and delivery #4 is marked as failed","when":"The failure event is processed","then":"The route is recalculated excluding delivery #4, re-optimizing the remaining stops from the driver's current position"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A driver's suggested next stop is Stop B but the driver goes to Stop D instead","when":"The system detects the deviation","then":"The route is recalculated from the driver's current position with remaining undelivered stops"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A driver completes a stop and the next 3 stops are deliveries for customers X, Y, Z","when":"The route advances","then":"Customers X, Y, Z each receive a notification that their delivery is arriving soon"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A motorcycle driver is in zone A and zone A has both parcel deliveries and a freight shipment","when":"The route is calculated","then":"The freight shipment is excluded from the motorcycle driver's route; Spec does not clarify."},{"scenario":"Two drivers in the same zone start shifts simultaneously","expected_behavior":"Deliveries must be partitioned between the two drivers, not duplicated. The system must handle concurrent route assignment without assigning the same delivery to multiple drivers."},{"scenario":"A delivery is added to a zone boundary — ambiguous zone membership","expected_behavior":"The system must have a deterministic zone resolution rule. A delivery belongs to exactly one zone."},{"scenario":"Driver's shift ends while deliveries remain on route","expected_behavior":"Undelivered stops must be returned to the pending pool for reassignment. They cannot be lost."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Route optimization directly impacts fuel costs, driver labor efficiency, and deliveries-per-shift throughput. A well-implemented system can reduce delivery costs 15-25%. Incorrect vehicle class assignment could result in failed deliveries, SLA penalties, and potential regulatory fines.","operational":"Dynamic recalculation adds system complexity but dramatically improves real-world route quality. Finding the right recalculation UX is critical.","risk":"Vehicle class constraint violation is the highest-risk failure mode — it is a safety and regulatory issue. Measurable KPIs: deliveries per shift, fuel cost per delivery, on-time delivery rate."}]},"confidence":{"level":72,"reasoning":"The spec is relatively clear and well-structured for a product feature description.
